mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending, proxied by real retail sales, signals strong aggregate demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns over the next month regardless of the economic regime.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of RSAFS on 20-day forward returns remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals accelerating labor market weakness that reduces consumer income and spending, leading to lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Median 60-day forward return after trigger is not negative, or the proportion of negative returns is below 55% over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and housing starts indicate broad-based economic expansion, as both supply and demand strengthen, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both have 3-month moving averages above their respective 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: Combined signal's hit rate (positive 60-day return when triggered) falls below 50% over any 3-year period or the average return is not significantly above zero.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPI y/y) exceeds 3% and the Fed raises rates by at least 25bp in one month, it signals aggressive tightening that compresses valuations and depresses equity prices over the following month; in low-inflation regimes the same rate hike has neutral effects.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year > 3% and FEDFUNDS rises by at least 25 basis points in one month
failure_condition: Hit rate of negative 20-day returns after trigger in high-inflation periods is below 55% over any 2-year window, or average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A strong payroll employment report (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer confidence and spending, which is immediately priced into equities and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward return following PAYEMS releases is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.10 over 10-year sample).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in housing starts (HOUST) by more than 5% signals a downturn in the residential investment sector, which historically precedes broader economic weakness and negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Proportion of negative 20-day returns following trigger is below 60% over any 5-year period, or the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls over a three-month period while the federal funds rate remains unchanged, it signals a 'Goldilocks' scenario of decreasing slack without monetary tightening, supporting sustained equity gains over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE decreasing over 3 consecutive months and FEDFUNDS unchanged over the same 3-month period
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward return after trigger is not positive and significant (t-test p < 0.05) over any 3-year window, or hit rate below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when industrial production is contracting year-over-year but retail sales are expanding, it indicates inventory depletion and imminent restocking, which boosts manufacturing activity and drives positive equity returns over the next month in supply-constrained environments.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year change < 0% and RSAFS year-over-year change > 0%
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return under the regime condition is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window, or hit rate below 55%.
